2605.22465.title: In Silico Modeling of the RAMPHO Buffer: Dissociating Informational and Energetic Masking via Phonetic Entropy in Deep Neural Networks
2605.22465.paper_summary: The fundamental challenge of listening in multi-talker environments is a cognitive bottleneck, defined by the Ease of Language Understanding (ELU) model as a failure within the RAMPHO episodic buffer. Current deep neural networks for speech enhancement optimize purely for physical acoustics, failing to account for the cognitive penalty of informational masking. Here, we present an in silico simulation of the RAMPHO buffer using the frame-by-frame phonetic entropy of a self-supervised acoustic model (wav2vec 2.0). By contrasting a semantically intact distractor with a phase-decorrelated distractor (the Concentration Shield) across a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) sweep, we successfully dissociate the cognitive penalty of informational distraction from the physical penalty of energetic decay. The simulation reveals a cognitive-acoustic Pareto optimization problem: destroying a distractor's semantic payload provides a release from informational masking at high SNRs, but fundamentally degrades temporal glimpsing cues at low SNRs.
2605.22465.paper_summary_zh: 多說話者環境中聆聽的根本挑戰是一種認知瓶頸，根據語言理解的易用性（ELU）模型，這被定義為RAMPHO情節緩衝區內的失敗。當前的語音增強深度神經網絡純粹針對物理聲學進行優化，未能考慮信息遮蔽的認知懲罰。在這裡，我們展示了一個使用自我監督聲學模型（wav2vec 2.0）的逐幀語音熵的RAMPHO緩衝區的計算機模擬。通過在信噪比（SNR）範圍內對比語義完整的干擾源與相位去相關的干擾源（集中盾），我們成功地將信息干擾的認知懲罰與能量衰減的物理懲罰區分開來。模擬揭示了一個認知-聲學的帕累托優化問題：破壞干擾源的語義負載在高SNR下提供了信息遮蔽的釋放，但在低SNR下基本上降低了時間瞬間提示的線索。
2605.22465.author: Stefan Bleeck et.al.
